sections.question: What forms must a candidate for city council use for his campaign filings, and what is required when the treasurer changes before the third pre-election statement?
sections.conclusion: As a recipient committee, a new treasurer must be appointed and Form 410 (Statement of Organization) must be filed with the Secretary of State reflecting this change. The committee must not receive or expend any funds until it has appointed a new treasurer. A post-election statement using Form 490 must be filed. The committee may only terminate when all conditions are met: the candidate has declared no intention to seek office, there is no surplus funds, and all required statements have been filed.

sections.question_synthetic: The inquiry asks what reporting forms and procedures a candidate must follow when the campaign treasurer terminates services before the third pre-election filing for a city council election.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that a new treasurer must be appointed and Form 410 filed immediately; no funds may be received or expended without a treasurer in place. The committee must file the required post-election statement and can only terminate after meeting specific criteria including filing all required disclosures and having no remaining surplus.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: OCR quality affects accuracy; document contains multiple embedded letters. Clear QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections present but some OCR errors in text (e.g., '1918' appears to be '1978'). Confidence reduced due to OCR artifacts and text quality issues in scanned document.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What forms must a candidate for city council use for his campaign filings, and what is required when the treasurer changes before the third pre-election statement?

CONCLUSION: As a recipient committee, a new treasurer must be appointed and Form 410 (Statement of Organization) must be filed with the Secretary of State reflecting this change. The committee must not receive or expend any funds until it has appointed a new treasurer. A post-election statement using Form 490 must be filed. The committee may only terminate when all conditions are met: the candidate has declared no intention to seek office, there is no surplus funds, and all required statements have been filed.

embedding.summary: FPPC response letter to South San Francisco City Clerk regarding campaign reporting requirements for a city council candidate whose treasurer terminated services before the third filing deadline, clarifying treasurer succession procedures and committee termination criteria.
